ddls:
CREATE TABLE "memory_regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory region */,
  "address_start" TEXT /* Starting address of the memory region */,
  "address_end" TEXT /* Ending address of the memory region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the memory region (e.g., Code, SRAM, Peripheral) */,
  "memory_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of memory (e.g., Normal, Device) */,
  "shareability" TEXT /* Shareability attribute of the memory region */,
  "write_policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the write policy (e.g., WBWA, WT) */,
  "cache_policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cache policy applied to the memory region */,
  "access_control" TEXT /* Access control settings for the memory region (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  "region_status" TEXT /* Status of the memory region (e.g., active, inactive, pending) */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification to the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_regions_memory_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("memory_type_id") REFERENCES memory_types ("memory_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_regions_write_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("write_policy_id") REFERENCES write_policies ("write_policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_types" (
  "memory_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the memory type */,
  "type_description" TEXT /* Description of the memory type */,
  "cacheable" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the memory type is cacheable */,
  "executable" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the memory type is executable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("memory_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "write_policies" (
  "write_policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each write policy */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the write policy */,
  "policy_description" TEXT /* Description of the write policy */,
  "default_policy" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if this is a default write policy */,
  "performance_impact" TEXT /* Description of the performance impact of the write policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("write_policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, engineer, auditor) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "account_status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, locked, disabled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, configure) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "default_role" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if this is a default role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration log entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory region being configured */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  "previous_value" TEXT /* Previous value of the configuration before the change */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the configuration after the change */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the change (e.g., performance optimization, bug fix) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_logs_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES memory_regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory region */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., read latency, write latency) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the performance metric (e.g., ms, s, MB/s) */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* Source of the performance metric (e.g., hardware sensor, software tool) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_performance_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES memory_regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date when the action was performed */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., login, logout, configure) */,
  "action_description" TEXT /* Description of the action */,
  "action_status" TEXT /* Status of the action (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "action_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "action_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
